summary: Steganographic loader executes hidden payload from public/assets/logo.png on require()
details: On module load, index.js walks up to 5 parent directories searching for public/assets/logo.png, scans the image bytes for the magic marker '__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__', base64-decodes everything after the marker, and executes the decoded string via new Function('require', code)(require) with full require() power. All errors are swallowed by a top-level try/catch so failures are silent. The advertised purpose ('Vite plugin to optimize and serve logo assets') has no need to parse or execute image contents; concealing executable JavaScript past a marker inside a PNG is a steganographic execution channel that defeats static review of the package itself, since the payload lives outside the npm tarball and can be swapped by anyone who can drop a logo.png into a consuming project. The package additionally exploits the 'vite-plugin-*' naming convention with a generic 'Vite Community' author to masquerade as a legitimate ecosystem plugin. Any Vite project that imports this plugin will execute attacker-controlled code at require time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
